2023-10-01.title: What Does a Russia-Leaning Party Win in an E.U. Nation Mean for Ukraine?

2023-10-02.abstract: Nearly all of the European Union’s top diplomats met in the Ukrainian capital, convening to reassert the bloc’s commitment against Russia’s invasion.

2023-10-02.abstract: The gathering in Kyiv, Ukraine, was described as the first time foreign ministers of the 27 E.U. nations met outside its borders.

2023-10-02.abstract: The storm is expected to graze China’s southern coast this weekend. It has drenched Taiwan and the Philippines.

2023-10-24.title: ‘Honest Mistake,’ Says U.S. Politician Arrested on Gun Charge in Hong Kong
2023-10-24.abstract: A Washington state senator, Jeff Wilson, said he discovered the pistol on his flight and notified customs officials at the Hong Kong airport.

2023-10-24.title: She Heeded Biden’s Warning to Migrants. Will She Regret It?
2023-10-24.abstract: After surviving a perilous jungle, a mother and daughter obeyed the president’s plea to enter the United States legally. 341 days later, they’re still waiting.

2023-10-25.title: A Close Look at Some Key Evidence in the Gaza Hospital Blast
2023-10-25.abstract: A widely cited missile video does not shed light on what happened, a Times analysis concludes.

2023-10-25.title: Women Will Vote at a Vatican Meeting for the First Time
2023-10-25.abstract: Some women participating in a conference on sensitive church issues said they feel heartened by the discussions taking place, though change may remain slow.

2023-10-25.title: As Ukraine War Grinds On, Widows Try to See Life After Loss
2023-10-25.abstract: Support groups formed to help thousands of new widows see a dual role in healing their nation. “Our society was not ready to face such scale of grief,” one widow said.

2023-10-25.title: A Hindu Goddess Festival Where Indian Progressivism Is Alive and Well
2023-10-25.abstract: A huge religious carnival shows that India remains a country of diverse political and social beliefs, despite the right-wing national government’s dominance.

2023-10-25.title: For a Grandmother in Gaza, History Is Scarier Than Airstrikes
2023-10-25.abstract: From his Los Angeles home, Mohammed Abujayyab has sought to help his grandmother and other relatives survive the Israeli bombardment. Memories of displacement are never far.

2023-10-26.title: Several Family Members of Al Jazeera’s Gaza Bureau Chief Are Killed
2023-10-26.abstract: Wael al-Dahdouh, described by his colleagues as “the voice of Palestinians in Gaza,” lost his wife, son, daughter and infant grandson, Al Jazeera said.

2023-10-26.title: Denmark Aims a Wrecking Ball at ‘Non-Western’ Neighborhoods
2023-10-26.abstract: A government program is using demolition and relocation to remake neighborhoods with immigrants, poverty or crime.

2023-10-26.title: South Korea Must Return Buddhist Statue to Japan, Supreme Court Says
2023-10-26.abstract: The artifact, which was taken to Tsushima centuries ago, was stolen and smuggled back to South Korea by thieves in 2012.

2023-10-26.title: Israel-Hamas War Protest Leads to Tense Scene at Cooper Union Library
2023-10-26.abstract: A video shared on social media showed pro-Palestinian protesters pounding on one side of locked library doors and Jewish students on the other.

2023-10-26.title: Alone and Outgunned, Israeli Police Fought Hamas From House to House
2023-10-26.abstract: Military support was slow to arrive in the quiet desert city of Ofakim but officers, most of them armed with handguns, fought back.

2023-10-27.title: Li Keqiang, Chinese Premier Eclipsed by Xi Jinping, Dies at 68
2023-10-27.abstract: Mr. Li, who retired in March, was muscled out of the premier’s usual policymaking roles by Mr. Xi. Chinese citizens expressed shock at his death, from a heart attack.

2023-10-27.title: Haunted by Guilt, Vilified Online: A Year After the Seoul Crowd Crush
2023-10-27.abstract: Survivors of the Itaewon disaster and relatives of victims continue to wrestle with unanswered questions and grief as they push for official accountability.

2023-10-27.title: Pentagon Says Video Shows Chinese Jet’s Close Approach to B-52
2023-10-27.abstract: The U.S. military called the near miss over the South China Sea an “unprofessional intercept.”

2023-10-27.title: Chinese Jet Flies Within 10 Feet of U.S. Bomber, Pentagon Says
2023-10-27.abstract: The fighter jet neared a B-52 during a maneuver over the South China Sea on Tuesday night, the U.S. military said. China had no immediate response.

2023-10-27.title: How Are You Preparing for a Summer of Fires?
2023-10-27.abstract: As El Niño arrives, heralding dry weather, it feels like the whole country is on edge. We’d like to hear from readers in Australia.

2023-10-28.title: ‘Let Gaza Live’: Calls for Cease-Fire Fill Grand Central Terminal
2023-10-28.abstract: A large demonstration, organized by Jewish Voice for Peace, came as Israel ramped up its military operations in Gaza.

2023-10-28.title: Chapter 2: Chasing Dreams at a Steep Cost
2023-10-28.abstract: She escaped an abusive betrothal, but her family’s reputation was suffering for it.

2023-10-28.title: The World Is Becoming More African
2023-10-28.abstract: Africa has the fastest growing, youngest population of any continent. By 2050, one in four people on the planet will be African. Early tremors of this seismic change are already registering around the world.

2023-10-28.title: Desperate for Air Defense, Ukraine Pushes U.S. for ‘Franken’ Weapons
2023-10-28.abstract: To meet the demand, the U.S. is producing so-called FrankenSAM systems that marry advanced Western weaponry with Soviet-era items still in Ukrainian stockpiles.

2023-10-28.title: Lured by Federal Dollars, Canadian Cities Rethink Zoning
2023-10-28.abstract: Major Canadian cities are opening up development zoning to include fourplexes in exchange for federal funds to build housing.

2023-10-29.title: Israel’s Hidden Victims, Arab Bedouins, Were Attacked by Hamas Too
2023-10-29.abstract: At least 17 died in the Hamas attacks, and many more in the impoverished community have since lost their livelihoods in Jewish farms that were ransacked.

2023-10-29.title: A Less Polarized Poland? Not Yet, Election Results Suggest.
2023-10-29.abstract: On whatever side they stand, many voters say they yearn for a more united nation. But after a brutal campaign, divisions — between left and right, urban and rural, young and old — remain stark.

2023-10-29.title: One Year After Tragedy, a Subdued Halloween in Itaewon
2023-10-29.abstract: The authorities in Seoul were out in force over the weekend, but the crowds in the typically bustling neighborhood were sparse.

2023-10-29.title: A New Place to Learn Civics: The Workplace
2023-10-29.abstract: Fearing that rising distrust could spell doom for businesses, some companies are offering employees lessons in democracy.

2023-10-29.title: Friends From Childhood. Brothers in War.
2023-10-29.abstract: Three friends met as boys in western Ukraine. When Russia invaded, they enlisted in the same unit. There was comfort, and dread, in serving together.

2023-10-30.title: War Through the Eyes of Gaza’s Children
2023-10-30.abstract: At a U.N. camp in southern Gaza, children have been forced to flee their homes and live in squalid conditions while trying to make sense of a war with no end in sight.

2023-10-30.title: Cornell Jewish Center Under Guard After Online Threats to Jewish Students
2023-10-30.abstract: “We will not tolerate antisemitism at Cornell,” the university president said after threats were made against the Center for Jewish Living.

2023-10-30.title: Driven Out of Pakistan, Afghans Face an Uncertain Future
2023-10-30.abstract: More than 70,000 undocumented Afghans have been forced across the border in recent weeks to meet a Wednesday deadline ordered by the Pakistani government.

2023-10-30.title: Both Sides Pay a Bloody Price for Coveted Ukrainian City
2023-10-30.abstract: The fight for the battered city of Avdiivka has emerged this fall as the fiercest battle of the war. Waves of Russian assaults have not broken through so far.

2023-10-30.title: Riot Over Plane From Israel Alarms Jews and Draws Condemnation
2023-10-30.abstract: A mob in the Russian republic of Dagestan stormed runways looking for a flight carrying Jewish passengers. The episode highlighted the threat of ethnic strife in Russia.

2023-10-31.title: King Charles, Visiting Kenya, Faces Calls to Answer for Colonial Abuses
2023-10-31.abstract: Older Kenyans who lived through the British colonial period want an apology and reparations. Younger Kenyans want an acknowledgment of more recent alleged abuses by British companies and troops.

2023-10-31.title: New Zealand Volcano Owner Is Found Guilty of Safety Failure
2023-10-31.abstract: The ruling brings an end to legal proceedings that began after the White Island eruption killed 22 people in 2019.

2023-10-31.title: After 8-Hour Standoff Near Tokyo, Hostages Are Free and Suspect Is Held
2023-10-31.abstract: The suspect, said by the Japanese media to be 86, was held after the police stormed a post office once two employees had reached safety.

2023-10-31.title: Hezbollah Hesitates as Israel Strikes Gaza
2023-10-31.abstract: After years of spoiling for a fight with Israel, the group is torn between maintaining its credibility as a defender of the Palestinians, and its hesitation to drag Lebanon into a full-scale war.

2023-10-31.title: In Cyberattacks, Iran Shows Signs of Improved Hacking Capabilities
2023-10-31.abstract: A monthslong hacking campaign targeted the governments of regional rivals, including Israel, and marked a turn, a new report says, as the attacks were used to collect intelligence, not just disrupt services.
